# mxcli Documentation

> CLI tool and MDL (Mendix Definition Language) for reading, querying, and modifying Mendix application projects headlessly. Designed primarily for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) and CI/CD pipelines.

Version: mxcli v0.10.0 @ 849fb4e | Generated: 2026-05-17T03:08:21Z

Full content (entire book, single page): https://www.mxcli.org/print.html

- [Preface](https://www.mxcli.org/preface/index.html): This documentation covers mxcli , a command-line tool for reading, querying, and modifying Mendix application projects, and MDL…
- [Vision](https://www.mxcli.org/preface/vision.html): mxcli and MDL exist to make Mendix development accessible to coding agents, automation pipelines, and developers who prefer…
- [What is mxcli?](https://www.mxcli.org/preface/what-is-mxcli.html): mxcli is a command-line tool that enables developers and AI coding assistants to read, understand, and modify Mendix application…
- [What is MDL?](https://www.mxcli.org/preface/what-is-mdl.html): MDL (Mendix Definition Language) is a text-based, SQL-like language for describing and manipulating Mendix application models. It…
- [Mendix Concepts for Newcomers](https://www.mxcli.org/preface/mendix-concepts.html): This page explains the core Mendix concepts you’ll encounter when using mxcli. If you’re familiar with web frameworks, relational…
- [Document Conventions](https://www.mxcli.org/preface/conventions.html): This page describes the formatting and notation conventions used throughout this documentation.

## Part I: Tutorial
- [5-Minute Quickstart](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/quickstart.html): Get from zero to modifying a Mendix project in 5 minutes. No prior MDL knowledge needed.
- [Setting Up](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/setup.html): Before you can start exploring and modifying Mendix projects from the command line, you need three things:
- [Installation](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/installation.html): There are three ways to install the VS Code MDL extension.
- [Opening Your First Project](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/opening-project.html): mxcli works with Mendix project files – the .mpr files that Mendix Studio Pro creates. Let’s open one.
- [The REPL](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/repl.html): The mxcli REPL is an interactive shell for working with Mendix projects, much like psql is for PostgreSQL or mysql for MySQL. You…
- [Exploring a Project](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/exploring.html): Once you have a project open – whether through the REPL, a CLI one-liner, or a script file – the next step is to look around…
- [SHOW MODULES, SHOW ENTITIES](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/show-commands.html): The SHOW family of commands lists project elements by type. They are the fastest way to see what a project contains.
- [DESCRIBE, SEARCH](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/describe-search.html): While SHOW commands list elements by name, DESCRIBE gives you the full definition of a single element. SEARCH lets you find…
- [SHOW STRUCTURE](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/show-structure.html): The SHOW STRUCTURE command gives you a compact, tree-style overview of a project. It is the fastest way to understand the overall…
- [Your First Changes](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/first-changes.html): Now that you can explore a project with SHOW and DESCRIBE , it’s time to make changes. This chapter walks through the three most…
- [Creating an Entity](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/create-entity.html): Entities are the foundation of any Mendix application – they define your data model. In this page you’ll create a Product entity…
- [Creating a Microflow](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/create-microflow.html): Microflows are the server-side logic of a Mendix application. They’re comparable to functions or methods in traditional…
- [Creating a Page](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/create-page.html): Pages are the user interface of a Mendix application. In this page you’ll create an overview page that lists products in a data…
- [Validating with mxcli check](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/validation.html): You’ve created entities, microflows, and pages. Before opening your project in Studio Pro, you should validate that everything is…
- [Working with AI Assistants](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/ai-assistants.html): mxcli is built from the ground up to work with AI coding assistants. The mxcli init command sets up your Mendix project with…
- [Claude Code Integration](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/claude-code.html): Claude Code is the primary AI integration for mxcli. It gets the deepest support: a dedicated configuration directory…
- [OpenCode Integration](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/opencode.html): OpenCode is a fully supported AI integration for mxcli. It gets deep support: a dedicated configuration directory, project-level…
- [Other AI tools](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/other-ai-tools.html): Claude Code is the default integration, but mxcli also supports OpenCode, Cursor, Continue.dev, Windsurf, Aider, and Mistral…
- [Skills and CLAUDE.md](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/skills.html): Skills are markdown files that teach AI assistants how to write correct MDL. Each skill covers a specific topic – creating pages…
- [The MDL + AI Workflow](https://www.mxcli.org/tutorial/mdl-ai-workflow.html): This page walks through the complete recommended workflow for using mxcli with an AI assistant, from project setup to reviewing…

## Part II: MDL by Example
- [MDL by Example](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/index.html): This part presents complete, self-contained MDL examples that show how the language comes together for real use cases. Each…
- [CRM Module](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/crm-module.html): A complete customer management feature: domain model, validation, CRUD pages, and security – all in one script.
- [REST Integration](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/rest-integration.html): Calling external APIs from microflows – GET, POST, authentication, and error handling.
- [Data Import Pipeline](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/data-import.html): Connect to an external database, explore its schema, import data into Mendix, and generate database connectors – all from the…
- [Master-Detail Page](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/master-detail.html): A single page with a list on the left and a detail form on the right. Selecting an item in the list updates the form via the…
- [Modifying Existing Pages](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/alter-page.html): ALTER PAGE modifies widgets in place without recreating the entire page. This is useful for incremental changes, maintenance, and…
- [Validation Pattern](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/validation.html): Validate an object before saving, showing feedback on invalid fields:
- [Role-Based Security](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/security.html): A complete security setup: module roles, entity access with XPath row-level constraints, document access, user roles, and demo…
- [View Entities](https://www.mxcli.org/examples/view-entities.html): View entities are read-only entities backed by an OQL query. They appear in the domain model but have no database table – their…

## Part III: Migration
- [Migration Guide](https://www.mxcli.org/migration/index.html): mxcli and MDL enable AI-assisted migration of existing applications to the Mendix platform. An AI coding agent (Claude Code…
- [Assessment](https://www.mxcli.org/migration/assessment.html)
- [Transformation Plan](https://www.mxcli.org/migration/transformation.html)
- [Generation](https://www.mxcli.org/migration/generation.html)
- [Data Migration](https://www.mxcli.org/migration/data-migration.html): mxcli can connect directly to external databases to explore schemas, import data, and generate database connector code. This is…
- [Validation and Handoff](https://www.mxcli.org/migration/validation.html)

## Part IV: The MDL Language
- [MDL Basics](https://www.mxcli.org/language/basics.html): MDL (Mendix Definition Language) is a SQL-like language for reading and modifying Mendix application projects. It provides a…
- [Lexical Structure](https://www.mxcli.org/language/lexical-structure.html): This page describes the tokens that make up the MDL language: keywords, literals, and identifiers.
- [Qualified Names](https://www.mxcli.org/language/qualified-names.html): MDL uses dot-separated qualified names to reference elements within a Mendix project. This naming convention ensures elements are…
- [Comments and Documentation](https://www.mxcli.org/language/comments.html): MDL supports three comment styles for annotating scripts and attaching documentation to model elements.
- [Script Files](https://www.mxcli.org/language/script-files.html): MDL statements can be saved in .mdl files and executed as scripts. This is the primary way to automate Mendix model changes.
- [Data Types](https://www.mxcli.org/language/data-types.html): MDL has a type system that maps to the Mendix metamodel attribute types. Every attribute in an entity definition has a type that…
- [Primitive Types](https://www.mxcli.org/language/primitive-types.html): This page documents each primitive type available for entity attributes in MDL.
- [Constraints](https://www.mxcli.org/language/constraints.html): Constraints must appear in this order:
- [Enumerations](https://www.mxcli.org/language/enumerations.html): Each enumeration contains:
- [Type Mapping](https://www.mxcli.org/language/type-mapping.html): External database types are mapped to Mendix types:
- [Domain Model](https://www.mxcli.org/language/domain-model.html): The domain model is the data layer of a Mendix application. It defines the entities (data tables), their attributes (columns)…
- [Entities](https://www.mxcli.org/language/entities.html): Entities are the primary data structures in a Mendix domain model. Each entity corresponds to a database table (for persistent…
- [Attributes and Validation Rules](https://www.mxcli.org/language/attributes.html): Attributes define the fields on an entity. Each attribute has a name, a type, and optional constraints.
- [Associations](https://www.mxcli.org/language/associations.html): Associations define relationships between entities. They determine how objects reference each other and control behavior on…
- [Generalization](https://www.mxcli.org/language/generalization.html): Generalization (inheritance) allows an entity to extend another entity, inheriting all of its attributes and associations. The…
- [Indexes](https://www.mxcli.org/language/indexes.html): Indexes improve query performance for frequently searched or sorted attributes. They are defined after the attribute list in an…
- [ALTER ENTITY](https://www.mxcli.org/language/alter-entity.html): Modifies an existing entity without full replacement.
- [Microflows and Nanoflows](https://www.mxcli.org/language/microflows.html): Microflows and nanoflows are the primary logic constructs in Mendix applications. They define executable sequences of activities…
- [Structure](https://www.mxcli.org/language/microflow-structure.html): An MPR file is a standard SQLite database with two key tables:
- [Activity Types](https://www.mxcli.org/language/activity-types.html): Workflow activities are the building blocks of a workflow definition. Each activity type serves a different purpose, from waiting…
- [Control Flow](https://www.mxcli.org/language/control-flow.html): MDL microflows support conditional branching, loops, and error handling to control the execution path of your logic.
- [Expressions](https://www.mxcli.org/language/expressions.html): Expressions in MDL are used in conditions, attribute assignments, variable assignments, and log messages within microflows. They…
- [Nanoflows vs Microflows](https://www.mxcli.org/language/nanoflows.html): Nanoflows are client-side logic flows that execute in the user’s browser or on mobile devices. They share the same MDL syntax as…
- [Common Patterns](https://www.mxcli.org/language/microflow-patterns.html): This page collects frequently used page patterns: overview/list pages, edit pages, and master-detail layouts. Each pattern is a…
- [Pages](https://www.mxcli.org/language/pages.html): MDL uses explicit property declarations for pages:
- [Page Structure](https://www.mxcli.org/language/page-structure.html): A page document has this top-level structure:
- [Widget Types](https://www.mxcli.org/language/widget-types.html): MDL supports a comprehensive set of widget types for building Mendix pages. Each widget is declared with a type keyword, a unique…
- [Data Binding](https://www.mxcli.org/language/data-binding.html): Data binding connects widgets to entity attributes and data sources. In MDL, binding is configured through widget properties…
- [Snippets](https://www.mxcli.org/language/snippets.html): Snippets are reusable page fragments that can be embedded in multiple pages. They allow you to define a widget tree once and…
- [ALTER PAGE / ALTER SNIPPET](https://www.mxcli.org/language/alter-page.html): Modify an existing page or snippet’s widget tree in-place without full CREATE OR REPLACE . Works directly on the raw BSON tree…
- [Common Patterns](https://www.mxcli.org/language/page-patterns.html): This page collects frequently used page patterns: overview/list pages, edit pages, and master-detail layouts. Each pattern is a…
- [Security](https://www.mxcli.org/language/security.html): Credentials are isolated from session output. The DSN (which contains username and password) is never displayed in session…
- [Module Roles and User Roles](https://www.mxcli.org/language/roles.html): Mendix security uses a two-tier role system. Module roles define permissions within a single module. User roles aggregate module…
- [Entity Access](https://www.mxcli.org/language/entity-access.html): Where <rights> is a comma-separated list of:
- [Microflow, Page, and Nanoflow Access](https://www.mxcli.org/language/document-access.html): Document-level access controls which module roles can execute microflows and nanoflows or view pages. Without an explicit grant…
- [GRANT / REVOKE](https://www.mxcli.org/language/grant-revoke.html): The GRANT and REVOKE statements control all permissions in a Mendix project. They work on three targets: entities (CRUD access)…
- [Demo Users](https://www.mxcli.org/language/demo-users.html): Demo users are test accounts created for development and testing. They appear on the login screen when demo users are enabled…
- [Navigation and Settings](https://www.mxcli.org/language/navigation.html): Navigation defines how users move through a Mendix application. Each device type has its own navigation profile with a home page…
- [Navigation Profiles](https://www.mxcli.org/language/navigation-profiles.html): A navigation profile defines the navigation structure for a specific device type. Each profile has a default home page, optional…
- [Home Pages and Menus](https://www.mxcli.org/language/home-pages.html): Each navigation profile has a default home page, optional role-specific home pages, and a menu tree. These determine what users…
- [Project Settings](https://www.mxcli.org/language/project-settings.html): Project settings control application runtime behavior, server configurations, language settings, and workflow configuration. MDL…
- [Workflows](https://www.mxcli.org/language/workflows.html): Workflow Activity Types:
- [Workflow Structure](https://www.mxcli.org/language/workflow-structure.html): A workflow definition consists of a context parameter, an optional overview page, and a sequence of activities. Activities…
- [Activity Types](https://www.mxcli.org/language/workflow-activities.html): Workflow activities are the building blocks of a workflow definition. Each activity type serves a different purpose, from waiting…
- [Workflow vs Microflow](https://www.mxcli.org/language/workflow-vs-microflow.html): Workflows and microflows are both used to express business logic, but they serve different purposes and have different execution…
- [Business Events](https://www.mxcli.org/language/business-events.html): Business events enable event-driven integration between Mendix applications. An application can publish events when something…
- [Event Services](https://www.mxcli.org/language/event-services.html): An event service is a named container for business event messages. It defines the contract between publishers and consumers: what…
- [Publishing and Consuming Events](https://www.mxcli.org/language/pub-sub-events.html): Business events follow a publish-subscribe pattern. One application publishes events when significant actions occur, and other…
- [Image Collections](https://www.mxcli.org/language/image-collections.html): Image collections are Mendix’s way of bundling images (icons, logos, graphics) within a module. Each collection can contain…

## Part V: Project Tools
- [Code Navigation](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/code-navigation.html): mxcli provides a suite of cross-reference navigation commands that let you explore relationships between elements in a Mendix…
- [SHOW CALLERS / CALLEES](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/callers-callees.html): These commands trace the call graph of your Mendix project, showing what calls a given element and what that element calls.
- [SHOW REFERENCES / IMPACT](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/references-impact.html): These commands provide broader reference tracking and impact analysis, helping you understand how changes propagate through a…
- [SHOW CONTEXT](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/show-context.html): The SHOW CONTEXT command assembles the surrounding context of an element within its module and project hierarchy. This is…
- [Full-Text Search](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/search.html): The SEARCH command performs full-text search across all strings and source code in a Mendix project. It searches element names…
- [Catalog Queries](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/catalog.html): The catalog is a SQLite-based query system that provides SQL access to project metadata. It indexes all elements in your Mendix…
- [REFRESH CATALOG](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/refresh-catalog.html): The REFRESH CATALOG command builds or rebuilds the catalog index from the current project state. The catalog must be refreshed…
- [Available Tables](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/catalog-tables.html): The catalog provides several tables that can be queried using standard SQL syntax. Use SHOW CATALOG TABLES to list all available…
- [SQL Queries](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/catalog-sql.html): The catalog supports standard SQL SELECT syntax for querying project metadata. Queries use the CATALOG. prefix to identify…
- [Use Cases](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/catalog-use-cases.html): After running IMPORT FROM , verify the data was imported correctly:
- [Linting and Reports](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/linting.html): mxcli includes an extensible linting framework that checks Mendix projects for best practice violations, security issues, naming…
- [Built-in Rules](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/builtin-rules.html): mxcli ships with 14 built-in lint rules implemented in Go. These rules are fast and always available.
- [Starlark Rules](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/starlark-rules.html): In addition to the built-in Go rules, mxcli bundles 27 Starlark-based lint rules. Starlark is a Python-like language that allows…
- [Writing Custom Rules](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/custom-rules.html): You can extend the linting framework by writing custom rules in Starlark, a Python-like language. Custom rules are placed in the…
- [mxcli lint](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/mxcli-lint.html): The mxcli lint command runs all lint rules (built-in Go rules and Starlark rules) against a Mendix project and reports findings.
- [mxcli report](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/mxcli-report.html): The mxcli report command generates a scored best practices report for a Mendix project. It runs all lint rules and aggregates…
- [Terminal UI (TUI)](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/tui.html): mxcli includes a terminal-based interactive UI for browsing, inspecting, and modifying Mendix projects. Built with Bubble Tea …
- [Navigation and Layout](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/tui-navigation.html): The TUI uses a Miller column layout for hierarchical browsing of Mendix projects. This design, inspired by file managers like…
- [Command Palette](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/tui-commands.html): Press : to open the command palette at the bottom of the TUI. The command bar provides VS Code-style command execution with tab…
- [MDL Execution](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/tui-execution.html): The TUI includes a built-in MDL execution view for running MDL statements directly from the terminal interface. Press x to open…
- [Project Validation](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/tui-check.html): The TUI integrates with mx check to validate Mendix projects directly from the terminal interface. Errors, warnings, and…
- [Session Management](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/tui-session.html): The TUI supports session persistence, allowing you to restore your previous browsing state when relaunching the tool.
- [Testing](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/testing.html): mxcli includes a testing framework for validating Mendix projects using MDL test files. Tests verify that MDL scripts execute…
- [Test Formats](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/test-formats.html): mxcli supports two test file formats: .test.mdl for pure MDL tests and .test.md for literate tests with documentation.
- [Test Annotations](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/test-annotations.html): Test annotations control test execution and define expectations. They are placed in comments within .test.mdl files.
- [Running Tests](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/running-tests.html): The mxcli test command executes test files and reports results.
- [Playwright Testing](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/playwright.html): mxcli integrates with playwright-cli for automated UI testing of Mendix applications. This enables verification that generated…
- [Diff](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/diff.html): mxcli provides two diff commands for comparing MDL scripts against project state and viewing local changes in MPR v2 projects.
- [External SQL](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/external-sql.html): mxcli can connect to external databases (PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server) for querying, schema exploration, data import, and…
- [SQL CONNECT](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/sql-connect.html): The SQL CONNECT command establishes a named connection to an external database.
- [Querying External Databases](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/sql-query.html): Once connected to an external database, you can explore its schema and run arbitrary SQL queries.
- [IMPORT FROM](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/import-from.html): The IMPORT command imports data from an external database into a Mendix application’s PostgreSQL database. It supports column…
- [Credential Management](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/credentials.html): mxcli provides secure credential management for external database connections. Credentials are isolated from session output…
- [Database Connector Generation](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/connector-generation.html): The SQL GENERATE CONNECTOR command auto-generates Mendix Database Connector MDL from an external database schema. This creates…
- [Docker Integration](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/docker.html): mxcli provides Docker integration for building, running, and validating Mendix applications without a local Mendix installation…
- [mxcli docker build](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/docker-build.html): The mxcli docker build command builds a Mendix application using mxbuild in Docker, including support for PAD (Platform-Agnostic…
- [mxcli docker check](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/docker-check.html): The mxcli docker check command validates a Mendix project without building it. It runs the Mendix mx check tool against the…
- [mxcli docker run](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/docker-run.html): The mxcli docker run command runs a Mendix application in a Docker container, providing a local runtime environment for testing…
- [OQL Queries](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/oql.html): The mxcli oql command executes OQL (Object Query Language) queries against a running Mendix runtime via the M2EE admin API. OQL…
- [Dev Container Setup](https://www.mxcli.org/tools/devcontainer.html): When using mxcli init , a .devcontainer/ configuration is also created. Opening the project in VS Code and choosing Reopen in…

## Part VI: IDE Integration
- [VS Code Extension](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/vscode.html): The MDL extension for VS Code provides a rich editing experience for .mdl files, bringing IDE-level support for Mendix Definition…
- [Installation](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/vscode-installation.html): There are three ways to install the VS Code MDL extension.
- [Syntax Highlighting and Diagnostics](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/vscode-syntax.html): The VS Code extension provides three layers of language feedback: syntax highlighting, parse diagnostics, and semantic…
- [Completion, Hover, Go-to-Definition](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/vscode-intellisense.html): The VS Code extension provides IntelliSense features powered by the MDL language server ( mxcli lsp --stdio ).
- [Project Tree](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/vscode-project-tree.html): The VS Code extension provides a Mendix Project Tree view that mirrors the App Explorer in Mendix Studio Pro, allowing you to…
- [Context Menu Commands](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/vscode-commands.html): The VS Code extension adds three commands to the editor context menu (right-click) for .mdl files. These commands provide quick…
- [LSP Server](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/lsp.html): The MDL language server implements the Language Server Protocol (LSP), enabling any editor with LSP support to provide rich MDL…
- [Protocol](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/lsp-protocol.html): The MDL language server uses the standard LSP stdio transport: JSON-RPC messages are exchanged over the process’s standard input…
- [Capabilities](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/lsp-capabilities.html): The MDL language server supports the following LSP capabilities.
- [Integration with Other Editors](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/lsp-editors.html): The MDL language server works with any editor that supports the Language Server Protocol. This page covers setup for editors…
- [mxcli init & mxcli new](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/mxcli-init.html)
- [What Gets Created](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/init-output.html): Running mxcli init (or mxcli new , which runs init automatically) creates the following directory structure in your Mendix…
- [Customizing Skills](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/customizing-skills.html): The skill files generated by mxcli init are starting points. You can modify them to match your organization’s patterns, naming…
- [Syncing with Updates](https://www.mxcli.org/ide/syncing.html): When you upgrade mxcli to a newer version, the skills, commands, and VS Code extension bundled with it may have changed. This…

## Part VII: Go Library
- [Quick Start](https://www.mxcli.org/library/quickstart.html): The modelsdk-go library provides programmatic access to Mendix projects from Go code. It is the underlying library that powers…
- [Installation](https://www.mxcli.org/library/installation.html): There are three ways to install the VS Code MDL extension.
- [Reading a Project](https://www.mxcli.org/library/reading.html): Open a Mendix project file ( .mpr ) in read-only mode to inspect its structure and contents.
- [Modifying a Project](https://www.mxcli.org/library/modifying.html): Make changes to a Mendix project file using the writer API. Always back up your .mpr file before modifying it.
- [Public API](https://www.mxcli.org/library/public-api.html): The public API surface is defined in modelsdk.go at the package root. It provides convenience functions for opening projects and…
- [Open / OpenForWriting](https://www.mxcli.org/library/open.html): The two entry points for accessing a Mendix project from Go code.
- [Reader Methods](https://www.mxcli.org/library/reader-methods.html): Complete list of methods available on the reader returned by modelsdk.Open() .
- [Writer Methods](https://www.mxcli.org/library/writer-methods.html): Complete list of methods available on the writer returned by modelsdk.OpenForWriting() .
- [Fluent API](https://www.mxcli.org/library/fluent-api.html): The api/ package provides a high-level, fluent builder API inspired by the Mendix Web Extensibility Model API. It simplifies…
- [ModelAPI Entry Point](https://www.mxcli.org/library/model-api.html): The api package provides a high-level fluent API inspired by the Mendix Web Extensibility Model API. It wraps the lower-level…
- [Builders](https://www.mxcli.org/library/builders.html): The api package provides fluent builder types for constructing Mendix model elements. Each builder follows the pattern: create…
- [Examples](https://www.mxcli.org/library/fluent-examples.html): Sample output:

## Part VIII: MDL Statement Reference
- [Connection Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/connection/index.html): Statements for opening and closing Mendix project files.
- [OPEN PROJECT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/connection/open-project.html)
- [CLOSE PROJECT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/connection/close-project.html)
- [Query Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/index.html): Statements for browsing and inspecting project elements. Query statements are read-only and never modify the project.
- [SHOW MODULES](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-modules.html)
- [SHOW ENTITIES](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-entities.html)
- [SHOW MICROFLOWS / NANOFLOWS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-microflows.html)
- [SHOW PAGES / SNIPPETS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-pages.html)
- [SHOW ENUMERATIONS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-enumerations.html)
- [SHOW ASSOCIATIONS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-associations.html)
- [SHOW CONSTANTS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-constants.html)
- [SHOW WORKFLOWS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-workflows.html)
- [SHOW BUSINESS EVENTS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-business-events.html)
- [SHOW IMAGE COLLECTION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-image-collection.html)
- [SHOW STRUCTURE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-structure.html): The SHOW STRUCTURE command gives you a compact, tree-style overview of a project. It is the fastest way to understand the overall…
- [SHOW WIDGETS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/show-widgets.html)
- [DESCRIBE ENTITY](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/describe-entity.html): To see the complete definition of an entity, including its attributes, types, and constraints:
- [DESCRIBE MICROFLOW / NANOFLOW](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/describe-microflow.html)
- [DESCRIBE PAGE / SNIPPET](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/describe-page.html)
- [DESCRIBE ENUMERATION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/describe-enumeration.html): Each value is followed by its caption (the display label shown to end users).
- [DESCRIBE ASSOCIATION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/describe-association.html)
- [SEARCH](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/query/search.html)
- [Domain Model Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/index.html): Statements for creating, altering, and dropping domain model elements: entities, enumerations, associations, and constants.
- [CREATE ENTITY](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/create-entity.html): The most common type. Data is stored in the application database:
- [ALTER ENTITY](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/alter-entity.html): Modifies an existing entity without full replacement.
- [DROP ENTITY](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/drop-entity.html): Removes an entity from the domain model:
- [CREATE ENUMERATION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/create-enumeration.html): Each value has an identifier (used in code) and a caption (displayed in the UI):
- [DROP ENUMERATION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/drop-enumeration.html): Remove an enumeration entirely:
- [CREATE ASSOCIATION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/create-association.html): A Reference association means each object on the FROM side can reference one object on the TO side. Multiple FROM objects can…
- [DROP ASSOCIATION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/drop-association.html): Remove an association:
- [CREATE CONSTANT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/domain-model/create-constant.html)
- [Microflow Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/microflow/index.html): Statements for creating and dropping microflows, nanoflows, and Java actions.
- [CREATE MICROFLOW](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/microflow/create-microflow.html)
- [CREATE NANOFLOW](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/microflow/create-nanoflow.html)
- [DROP MICROFLOW / NANOFLOW](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/microflow/drop-microflow.html)
- [CREATE JAVA ACTION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/microflow/create-java-action.html)
- [Page Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/page/index.html): Statements for creating, modifying, and dropping pages, snippets, and layouts.
- [CREATE PAGE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/page/create-page.html): The basic syntax for creating a page:
- [CREATE SNIPPET](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/page/create-snippet.html): A snippet without parameters:
- [ALTER PAGE / ALTER SNIPPET](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/page/alter-page.html): Modify an existing page or snippet’s widget tree in-place without full CREATE OR REPLACE . Works directly on the raw BSON tree…
- [DROP PAGE / SNIPPET](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/page/drop-page.html)
- [CREATE LAYOUT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/page/create-layout.html)
- [Security Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/security/index.html): Statements for managing project security: module roles, user roles, entity access rules, microflow and page access, demo users…
- [CREATE MODULE ROLE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/security/create-module-role.html)
- [CREATE USER ROLE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/security/create-user-role.html)
- [GRANT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/security/grant.html)
- [REVOKE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/security/revoke.html)
- [CREATE DEMO USER](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/security/create-demo-user.html)
- [Navigation Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/navigation/index.html): Statements for configuring application navigation profiles, home pages, login pages, and menu structures.
- [ALTER NAVIGATION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/navigation/alter-navigation.html)
- [SHOW NAVIGATION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/navigation/show-navigation.html)
- [Workflow Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/workflow/index.html): Statements for creating, inspecting, and dropping workflows.
- [CREATE WORKFLOW](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/workflow/create-workflow.html)
- [DROP WORKFLOW](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/workflow/drop-workflow.html)
- [OData and Integration Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/odata/index.html): Statements for managing OData services, external entities, and browsing service contracts.
- [Integration Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/integration/index.html): Statements for managing JSON structures, import mappings, export mappings, and data transformers.
- [CREATE JSON STRUCTURE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/integration/create-json-structure.html)
- [CREATE IMPORT MAPPING](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/integration/create-import-mapping.html)
- [CREATE EXPORT MAPPING](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/integration/create-export-mapping.html)
- [CREATE DATA TRANSFORMER](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/integration/create-data-transformer.html)
- [Agent Editor Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/agent/index.html): Statements for managing AI agent editor documents. Requires Mendix 11.9+ and the AgentEditorCommons module.
- [CREATE MODEL](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/agent/create-model.html)
- [CREATE KNOWLEDGE BASE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/agent/create-knowledge-base.html)
- [CREATE CONSUMED MCP SERVICE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/agent/create-consumed-mcp-service.html)
- [CREATE AGENT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/agent/create-agent.html)
- [Business Event Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/business-event/index.html): Statements for managing business event services.
- [CREATE BUSINESS EVENT SERVICE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/business-event/create-business-event-service.html): Message attributes support standard Mendix types:
- [DROP BUSINESS EVENT SERVICE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/business-event/drop-business-event-service.html): Remove an event service:
- [Image Collection Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/image-collection/index.html): Statements for creating, inspecting, and dropping image collections within modules.
- [CREATE IMAGE COLLECTION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/image-collection/create-image-collection.html): The image format is detected automatically from the file extension. Relative paths are resolved from the current working…
- [DROP IMAGE COLLECTION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/image-collection/drop-image-collection.html): Remove a collection and all its embedded images:
- [SHOW / DESCRIBE IMAGE COLLECTION](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/image-collection/show-describe-image-collection.html)
- [Catalog Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/catalog/index.html): The catalog is a SQLite database that caches project metadata for fast querying and cross-reference navigation. It is stored in…
- [REFRESH CATALOG](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/catalog/refresh-catalog.html): The REFRESH CATALOG command builds or rebuilds the catalog index from the current project state. The catalog must be refreshed…
- [SELECT FROM CATALOG](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/catalog/select-from-catalog.html)
- [SHOW CALLERS / CALLEES](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/catalog/show-callers-callees.html): These commands trace the call graph of your Mendix project, showing what calls a given element and what that element calls.
- [SHOW REFERENCES / IMPACT / CONTEXT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/catalog/show-references-impact.html)
- [SHOW CATALOG TABLES](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/catalog/show-catalog-tables.html)
- [External SQL Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/external-sql/index.html): Direct SQL query execution against external databases (PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server). Credentials are isolated – DSN never…
- [SQL CONNECT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/external-sql/sql-connect.html): The SQL CONNECT command establishes a named connection to an external database.
- [SQL DISCONNECT](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/external-sql/sql-disconnect.html)
- [SQL (query)](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/external-sql/sql-query.html)
- [SQL GENERATE CONNECTOR](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/external-sql/sql-generate-connector.html)
- [IMPORT FROM](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/external-sql/import-from.html): The IMPORT command imports data from an external database into a Mendix application’s PostgreSQL database. It supports column…
- [Settings Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/settings/index.html): Statements for viewing and modifying Mendix project settings. Settings are organized into categories: MODEL (application-level…
- [SHOW SETTINGS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/settings/show-settings.html)
- [ALTER SETTINGS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/settings/alter-settings.html): Settings are organized into categories. Each ALTER SETTINGS command targets one category.
- [Organization Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/organization/index.html): Statements for organizing project structure: creating modules and folders, and moving documents between them.
- [CREATE MODULE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/organization/create-module.html)
- [CREATE FOLDER](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/organization/create-folder.html)
- [DROP FOLDER](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/organization/drop-folder.html)
- [MOVE](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/organization/move.html)
- [MOVE FOLDER](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/organization/move-folder.html)
- [JAR Dependency Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/jar-dependency/index.html): Statements for managing Maven/JAR dependencies declared in a module’s settings.
These map to the Module Settings → Java…
- [ALTER MODULE JAR DEPENDENCY](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/jar-dependency/alter-module-jar-dependency.html)
- [LIST / DESCRIBE JAR DEPENDENCY](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/jar-dependency/list-describe-jar-dependency.html)
- [Session Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/session/index.html): Statements for inspecting and configuring the current REPL session.
- [SET](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/session/set.html)
- [SHOW STATUS](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/session/show-status.html)

## Part IX: Architecture & Internals
- [System Architecture](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/architecture.html): ModelSDK Go is a Go-native library for reading and modifying Mendix application projects ( .mpr files). It provides programmatic…
- [Layer Diagram](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/layers.html): Detailed description of each architectural layer in ModelSDK Go.
- [Package Structure](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/packages.html): Overview of the Go package organization and dependency relationships.
- [Design Decisions](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/design-decisions.html): Key architectural decisions made during the development of ModelSDK Go, with rationale and trade-offs.
- [MPR File Format](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/mpr-format.html): Mendix projects are stored in .mpr files, which are SQLite databases containing BSON-encoded model elements. This page provides…
- [v1 vs v2](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/mpr-v1-v2.html)
- [BSON Document Structure](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/bson-structure.html): How Mendix project documents are serialized in BSON format, including the role of $Type fields, nested structures, array version…
- [Storage Names vs Qualified Names](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/storage-names.html): Mendix uses different “storage names” in BSON $Type fields than the “qualified names” shown in the TypeScript SDK documentation…
- [Widget Template System](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/widget-templates.html): Pluggable widgets (DataGrid2, ComboBox, Gallery, etc.) require embedded template definitions for correct BSON serialization. This…
- [Pluggable Widget Engine](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/widget-engine.html): The Pluggable Widget Engine replaces hardcoded Go builder functions with a data-driven system. Widget behavior is described in…
- [MDL Parser](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/parser.html): The MDL parser translates SQL-like MDL (Mendix Definition Language) syntax into executable operations against Mendix project…
- [ANTLR4 Grammar Design](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/antlr4-grammar.html): The MDL parser uses ANTLR4 with separate lexer and parser grammars. This page covers the design patterns and key decisions in the…
- [Lexer - Parser - AST - Executor Pipeline](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/parser-pipeline.html): The complete parsing pipeline from raw MDL text through lexical analysis, parsing, AST construction, and execution against the…
- [Adding New Statements](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/adding-statements.html): Step-by-step guide for adding a new MDL statement to the parser and executor.
- [Catalog System](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/catalog.html): The catalog is an in-memory SQLite database that indexes Mendix project metadata for fast querying, full-text search, and…
- [SQLite Schema](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/catalog-schema.html): The catalog uses an in-memory SQLite database with the following table definitions.
- [FTS5 Full-Text Search](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/fts5.html): The catalog uses SQLite’s FTS5 extension to provide fast full-text search across all project content.
- [Reference Tracking](https://www.mxcli.org/internals/reference-tracking.html): The cross-reference tracking system powers the callers, callees, references, and impact analysis queries. It is built during…

## Guides
- [Capabilities Overview](https://www.mxcli.org/reference/capabilities.html): Everything mxcli can do, organized by use case.
- [Customizing AI Generation](https://www.mxcli.org/guides/customizing-ai.html): How to make AI assistants (Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor, etc.) generate Mendix code that follows your team’s conventions.

## Part X: Appendixes
- [MDL Quick Reference](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/quick-reference.html): Complete syntax reference for MDL (Mendix Definition Language). This is the authoritative reference for all MDL statement syntax.
- [Data Type Mapping Table](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/data-type-mapping.html): Comprehensive mapping between MDL data types, Mendix internal types, and backend representations.
- [Reserved Words](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/reserved-words.html): Complete reference for MDL reserved words and quoting rules.
- [Mendix Version Compatibility](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/version-compatibility.html): Supported Mendix Studio Pro versions, feature availability matrix, and known limitations.
- [Common Mistakes and Anti-Patterns](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/common-mistakes.html): Frequent errors encountered when writing MDL scripts or using the SDK, with explanations of why they occur and how to avoid them.
- [Error Messages Reference](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/error-messages.html): Common error messages from Studio Pro, mxcli, and the MDL parser, with explanations and solutions.
- [Glossary](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/glossary.html): Mendix-specific terms and concepts for developers who are new to the Mendix platform.
- [TypeScript SDK Equivalence](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/sdk-equivalence.html): Mapping between the official Mendix Model SDK (TypeScript) and MDL/modelsdk-go equivalents.
- [Changelog](https://www.mxcli.org/appendixes/changelog.html): Version history and release notes for ModelSDK Go and the mxcli tool.

## Blog
- [Blog](https://www.mxcli.org/blog/index.html): News, release announcements, and demos from the mxcli team.
- [Introducing mxcli](https://www.mxcli.org/blog/introduction.html)
